meta.reportLabel: Fri Report — PCE confirmed HOT (core 3.4% / hdline 4.1%); dollar long validated, DXY 13-mo high (~101.4); GBP re-armed (services 3.7%); Brent 4-mo low (~$74.4)
meta.coverage: USD, EUR, GBP, JPY, AUD, NZD, CAD · 18 pairs + DXY + JPYBASKET + GER40
meta.regime: Dollar-strong, validated. May core PCE printed 3.4% (up from 3.3%) and headline 4.1% (up from 3.8%) — the hottest core since Oct 2023 — confirming Warsh's 'broader than energy' call and validating the crowded dollar long rather than fading it. DXY consolidates a 13-mo high (~101.4); ~63% Sept Fed hike still priced. GBP stays re-armed (services CPI 3.7%); Brent at a 4-mo low ~$74.4 keeps CAD pressed. USD/JPY ~161.5 (1986 low), squeezed. Focus rotates to quarter-end flows (Jun 30) and June jobs (Jul 2).
meta.nextBigEvent: Quarter-end rebalancing (Jun 30) then US June jobs / NFP (Jul 2, 08:30 EDT) — the next dollar test

dailyRead: The marquee resolved hawkish: May core PCE printed 3.4% (up from 3.3%) and headline 4.1% (up from 3.8%) — the hottest core since Oct 2023 — confirming Warsh's 'broader than energy' call and validating the crowded dollar long rather than fading it. Because the number landed broadly in line, DXY consolidated at a 13-month high (~101.4) instead of spiking, and Sept-hike odds eased only modestly to ~63%; the binary risk is now behind us and resolved the dollar's way, so the USD-divergence legs trade cleaner. GBP stays re-armed (May services CPI 3.7% vs 3.2%) and Brent slid further to a 4-month low ~$74.4 (-19% m/m), keeping CAD pressed and GER40 supported. The cleanest trades are the now-confirmed USD-divergence legs (EUR/USD short, USD/CAD long, EUR/JPY short); the structural long-JPY core (USD/JPY short, JPYBASKET long) remains squeezed near a 1986 low (~161.5) — defend, do not add. Focus now rotates to quarter-end flows (Jun 30) and the June jobs report (Jul 2) as the next dollar test.

strength.drivers: Core PCE 3.4% (May, up from 3.3%); headline PCE 4.1%; CPI 4.2%; NFP +172k. Fed 3.50-3.75%, hawkish hold, dots flip next move to a HIKE.
strength.forward: 🦅 Confirmed & strengthening: hot May PCE validates Warsh; ~63% on a Sept hike; DXY 13-mo high ~101.4. The binary is behind us — Jul 2 jobs is the next fade. Next FOMC late Jul.

strength.drivers: Core 2.6% (May); services CPI 3.7% (up from 3.2%, fastest in months); CPI 2.8% (held). BoE 3.75%, hawkish 7-2 hold; sees CPI ~3.25% by Q4.
strength.forward: 🦅 Hawkish — services re-accelerated to 3.7%, keeping the cut-tail shut and the rate story alive. Risk: a soft activity wave re-splits it. Next BoE Aug.

strength.drivers: May CPI: trimmed mean 3.6%; headline ~4.0% with a ~4.8% Q2 peak expected; unemp 4.5%. RBA 4.35%, hawkish hold, on hold to year-end.
strength.forward: ⚖️🦅 Sticky 3.6% trim + a 4.8% Q2 peak arm an Aug-hike case, but a confirmed-hot-PCE USD keeps AUD/USD sub-0.70. Risk: soft Jul 23 jobs. Next RBA Aug.

strength.drivers: Headline easing (peak ~4.3% Q3 exp.); recovery establishing. RBNZ 2.25%; Q1 GDP revised firm +1.0% q/q. Markets price a 25bp hike Jul 9.
strength.forward: ⚖️🦅 Firmer Q1 GDP (+1.0%) + a priced Jul RBNZ hike floor NZD; hawkish USD caps NZD/USD. Net firm-but-capped into Jul 9 RBNZ.

strength.drivers: Core ~1.5%; PPI hot. BoJ 1.00% (hiked Jun 16), Summary of Opinions favours more hikes; spot ~161.5 (near 1986 low).
strength.forward: 🦅↔ Destination unchanged, spot squeezed. Katayama-Bessent reaffirmed US-Japan COORDINATION readiness, but markets doubt Tokyo's will after April's reserve-draining op — two-sided, the swing. Next BoJ late Jul.

strength.drivers: May CPI 3.2% (up from 2.8%); trimmed core ~2.0-2.5%. BoC 2.25% (4th hold, Jun 10), looking through energy (sees ~3% near-term).
strength.forward: ⚖️🔻 Hot/broadening CPI gives CAD a floor; Brent's slide to a 4-mo low (~$74.4, -19% m/m) keeps the export side pressed; BoC looks through energy. Next BoC late Jul.

strength.drivers: Core ~2.5% ('26 proj); headline 3.0% '26 proj; unemp ~6.3%. ECB 2.25% (hiked Jun 11), framed as one-off war insurance, not a cycle; growth cut to 0.8%.
strength.forward: ⚖️🔻 Pure USD-leg: a confirmed-hot-PCE Fed pushes EUR/USD down, dovish-frame ECB caps bounces. EZ PMI downturn easing is not a turn. Next ECB late Jul.

symbols.why: Cleaner post-PCE: the binary that could've bounced it resolved hawkish (core 3.4%) — hawkish-Fed vs an ECB that called its hike one-off war insurance. DXY 13-mo high.
symbols.risk: Very crowded; quarter-end (Jun 30) flows can squeeze; soft Jul 2 jobs.

symbols.why: Both legs aligned: hot US PCE + Brent 4-mo low (~$74.4, -19% m/m). Broadening CPI only a floor (core ~2.0%) as BoC looks through it.
symbols.risk: An oil re-spike (MOU stall / Lebanon) rescues CAD; quarter-end USD selling.

symbols.why: Weak yen HELPS this: structural BoJ-tightening destination + a capped, dovish-frame EUR — cleaner than the USD/JPY leg.
symbols.risk: A coordination-flavoured intervention spike snaps every XXX/JPY lower at once.

symbols.why: Structural core (BoJ hiking, coord watch) but SQUEEZED ~161.5 (1986 low). Defend, do NOT add here.
symbols.risk: Tokyo silent + clean break = momentum higher; a JOINT intervention spike is the violent re-load.

symbols.why: USD headwind dominant: hot PCE + Fed repricing keep it sub-0.70, overpowering the firm trim-mean CPI.
symbols.risk: The sticky 3.6% core + a soft Jul 2 NFP flips the leg — the firm-AUD invalidation.

symbols.risk: Soft Jul 2 jobs lifts it; soft UK activity re-splits it.

symbols.why: Firm Q1 GDP + priced Jul RBNZ hike floor NZD; hawkish USD caps it — net neutral into Jul 9.
symbols.risk: Risk-on lifts NZD; a soft US jobs print helps.

symbols.why: Services-CPI re-acceleration firmed the GBP leg, offsetting the yen destination — two-sided.
symbols.risk: Firm GBP + weak yen spot tilts it up; a coordinated intervention spike snaps it lower.

symbols.why: Weak yen leans it down, but risk-off carry unwind + RBA floor make it modest only.
symbols.risk: Risk-on carry holds it up; a coordinated spike is the abrupt re-load.

symbols.why: Structural JPY destination vs an NZD floored by firm GDP — carry offsets, low conviction.
symbols.risk: Risk-on carry dominates near-term -> stalls; an intervention spike re-loads.

symbols.why: ECB done-dovish vs a hawkish-hold RBA (sticky trim mean) — mild AUD tilt, no USD vector.
symbols.risk: EZ vs Aussie data set direction.

symbols.why: ECB spent vs an NZD floored by firm GDP + Jul-hike pricing — slight NZD tilt.

symbols.why: Two firm legs: re-armed BoE (services 3.7%) vs hawkish-hold RBA (3.6% trim) — no clean edge.
symbols.risk: UK services vs Aussie sticky-core split sets direction.

symbols.why: Hawkish-re-armed GBP vs an NZD floored by firm GDP — both firm, no divergence.

symbols.why: Both commodity-bloc, both floored (RBA sticky-core vs RBNZ Jul-hike priced) — a wash.
symbols.risk: Aussie data vs RBNZ Jul 9.

symbols.why: Upgraded: hot PCE confirmed the hawkish leg, so the 13-mo high (~101.4) is validated, not just crowded; EUR/JPY/GBP/CAD legs all soft vs USD.

catalysts.note: Q2 close; rebalancing flows can squeeze the crowded dollar long regardless of macro — the nearest tactical risk.

catalysts.event: US June jobs report (NFP) — moved up for Jul 4
catalysts.note: Next dollar test; May was +172k (beat). A soft print is the cleanest fade of the validated dollar long.
25bp hike priced; firm Q1 GDP backs it — the NZD swing.
Tests whether energy pass-through broadens; BoC looks through for now.
USD/JPY ~161.5; Katayama-Bessent reaffirmed coordinated-action readiness, though markets doubt follow-through — biggest near-term JPY-book swing.
Brent at a 4-mo low ~$74.4 on eased Hormuz flows + peace progress; stalled talks (Lebanon flare) keep a respike tail.
All next decisions parked to late July.

geopolitics: Flows are still crushing the war premium. With Strait-of-Hormuz tanker traffic normalising and US-Iran peace progress holding, Brent slipped to ~$74.4 on Friday — a fresh 4-month low, down ~19% on the month and roughly 40% off the wartime peak. The diplomatic track remains stalled (the technical round is still cancelled amid the re-erupted Lebanon fighting), so the base case (truce holds, oil drifts lower) is intact, but the slipped talks keep the respike tail alive. Transmission: oil → energy/headline inflation → CB hawkishness → FX. Base case keeps the dollar bid, supports GER40 and risk-beta AUD/NZD on calmer days, and does NOT buy a Fed cut (hot May PCE confirmed inflation marked up into the oil drop; disinflation is energy-led, not broad); for CAD the hot CPI (3.2%) only partly offsets the deeper oil drag → soft-and-capped at ~$74. Tail (alive): an MOU stall / Lebanon escalation re-spikes oil (rescues CAD, hurts AUD/NZD/GER40, hands USD & JPY competing haven bids) — the persistent ~$4,200 gold bid is the thin premium for exactly this.
